message:
chore(beads): close bd-26w after implementation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1ia","title":"On-demand message summarization with stored history","description":"Issue #80 idea 3: On-demand message summarization with optional periodic scheduling. The project ALREADY has summarization infrastructure: _compute_thread_summary() in app.py, summarize_thread() tool, LiteLLM integration (llm.py), and cost tracking. This bead extends that to project-wide time-windowed summaries.

SCOPE: On-demand summarization first (MVP). Periodic background jobs deferred to follow-up bead.

DESIGN DECISIONS:
- Leverage existing _compute_thread_summary() and _summarize_messages() internals
- Use existing LiteLLM integration (llm.py) with cost tracking and cache support
- Store summaries in new table for retrieval (not computed on-the-fly each time)
- On-demand via new MCP tool: summarize_recent(project_key, since_hours=1)
- Periodic scheduling deferred (would require APScheduler, significant new dependency)

Implementation:

1. Database schema (new table):
   CREATE TABLE message_summaries (
     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     project_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id),
     summary_text TEXT NOT NULL,
     start_ts REAL NOT NULL,
     end_ts REAL NOT NULL,
     source_message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
     source_thread_ids TEXT,  -- JSON array of thread IDs included
     llm_model TEXT,
     cost_usd REAL,
     created_ts REAL NOT NULL
   );
   CREATE INDEX idx_summaries_project_ts ON message_summaries(project_id, end_ts);

2. New MCP tool: summarize_recent(project_key, since_hours=1, model=None):
   - Fetch all messages in project from last N hours
   - Group by thread for context
   - Call existing _compute_thread_summary() per thread, or batch if > 10 threads
   - Combine thread summaries into project-wide summary
   - Store in message_summaries table
   - Return summary text + metadata (message count, cost, threads covered)
   - Idempotent: if summary exists for same time window (within 5-min tolerance), return cached

3. New MCP tool: fetch_summary(project_key, since_hours=24, limit=5):
   - Retrieve stored summaries from message_summaries table
   - Filter by time window
   - Return most recent summaries with metadata
   - New resource: resource://summaries/{project}?since=24h&limit=5

4. Cost guardrails:
   - Max messages per summarization: 500 (prevent runaway costs)
   - Warn if estimated cost > $0.10 per summarization
   - Track cumulative cost in existing cost tracking system
   - Default model: use cheapest available (haiku/flash tier)

5. Summary format:
   - Structured output: key_decisions[], blockers_resolved[], work_completed[], open_questions[]
   - Plain text fallback if structured parsing fails
   - Include source thread IDs for traceability

EDGE CASES:
- No messages in time window: return "No activity in the last N hours" (no LLM call)
- Very large message volume (500+): truncate to most recent 500, note truncation in summary
- LLM API failure: return partial summary from thread-level summaries, or error with retry guidance
- Cost exceeds guardrail: warn but proceed (user configured the time window)
- Overlapping time windows: each summary is independent, no dedup needed
- Summary retention: keep for 90 days, then auto-delete (configurable)

TESTS:

Unit Tests (tests/test_summarize_recent.py):
- test_summarize_empty_window: no messages -> "No activity" response, no LLM call
- test_summarize_single_thread: 1 thread with 5 messages -> summary generated
- test_summarize_multiple_threads: 3 threads -> combined summary
- test_summarize_stores_in_db: after summarization, message_summaries table has new row
- test_summarize_idempotent: same time window twice -> returns cached (no duplicate LLM call)
- test_summarize_cost_tracking: cost_usd populated in summary record
- test_summarize_max_messages: > 500 messages -> truncated to 500, note in summary
- test_fetch_summary_by_time: fetch_summary with since_hours filter returns matching summaries
- test_fetch_summary_limit: limit=3 returns at most 3 summaries
- test_summary_retention_cleanup: summaries older than 90 days removed on cleanup

Integration Tests:
- test_summarize_then_fetch: create summary -> fetch_summary returns it
- test_summarize_with_llm_mock: mock LiteLLM -> verify prompt includes message content
- test_summarize_structured_output: verify key_decisions, blockers_resolved fields populated

E2E Tests:
- test_full_summary_workflow: send 10 messages across 3 threads -> summarize_recent -> fetch_summary -> verify content
- test_summary_resource_endpoint: resource://summaries/project?since=1h returns valid JSON

GitHub issue: #80","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:11:06.099402944Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:58.716267608Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":11,"issue_id":"bd-1ia","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"EXISTING CODE TO REUSE:
- _compute_thread_summary() in app.py — core summarization logic
- summarize_thread() tool — existing MCP tool for single-thread summaries  
- test_summarize_threads_extended.py — multi-thread test patterns
- test_summarize_threads_llm_off.py — non-LLM mode patterns (for cost-free testing)
- test_summarize_threads_llm_on.py — LLM mode with cost tracking patterns
- complete_system_user() in llm.py — async LLM completion helper

IMPLEMENTATION PATTERN: Follow the same pattern as summarize_thread() but with time-windowed message collection instead of thread-based collection.

ADDITIONAL TEST LOGGING REQUIREMENTS:
All tests should log:
  - Time window: 'Summarizing messages from T1 to T2 (N messages across M threads)'
  - Thread grouping: 'Grouped into threads: [thread-1 (5 msgs), thread-2 (3 msgs), ...]'
  - LLM call details: 'Calling {model} with {N} tokens, estimated cost ${X}'
  - Summary storage: 'Stored summary {id} covering {N} messages, cost=${X}'
  - Cache hits: 'Returning cached summary {id} (created {T}, covers same window)'
  - Idempotency: 'Existing summary found within 5-min tolerance, returning cached'
  - Truncation: 'Truncating from {N} to 500 messages (oldest {M} dropped)'

For cost-sensitive tests, use LLM_OFF mode to avoid real API calls:
  @pytest.fixture
  def llm_off_env(monkeypatch):
      monkeypatch.setenv('AGENT_MAIL_LLM_MODE', 'off')
      
This ensures tests run fast and free while still testing the full pipeline.
","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:58Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tz","title":"Persistent window-based agent identity system","description":"Issue #80 idea 1: Agents should get persistent identities tied to their tmux/terminal window, surviving session restarts. This eliminates per-session identity registration overhead and enables tracking which window/pane is doing what in multi-agent grids.

DESIGN DECISIONS:
- Env var name: MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID (NOT LIQUID_MAIL — project is mcp_agent_mail)
- Identity format: UUID (simple, portable, debuggable) with optional display-name mapping
- Display names: adjective-noun-hash format (reuse existing _generate_unique_agent_name() pattern from app.py:4500+)
- Lifecycle: window identity persists until explicitly deleted or TTL expires (default 30 days)
- Auto-registration: agents automatically inherit window identity when register_agent() called without explicit name

Implementation:

1. Database schema (new table):
   CREATE TABLE window_identities (
     id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
     project_id TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES projects(id),
     window_uuid TEXT NOT NULL,
     display_name TEXT NOT NULL,
     created_ts REAL NOT NULL,
     last_active_ts REAL NOT NULL,
     expires_ts REAL,
     UNIQUE(project_id, window_uuid)
   );

2. Environment variable handling (config.py):
   - Read MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID from environment
   - If set, use as window_uuid for identity lookup/creation
   - If not set, generate new UUID and suggest user export it

3. Identity resolution in register_agent() (app.py):
   - If MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID is set:
     a. Look up window identity in DB
     b. If found, use existing display_name as agent name
     c. If not found, create new window identity with generated display_name
     d. Update last_active_ts on every registration
   - If not set: use current behavior (explicit name or auto-generate)

4. Include identity in message responses:
   - Add "window_id" and "window_display_name" fields to message metadata
   - Agents can see which window sent each message

5. New MCP tools:
   - list_window_identities(project_key) -> list of active window identities with names, last_active, age
   - rename_window(project_key, window_uuid, new_display_name) -> update display name
   - expire_window(project_key, window_uuid) -> mark window as expired

6. Cleanup: periodic task or on-demand to remove expired windows (> 30 days inactive)

EDGE CASES:
- Window ID collision: UUID makes this astronomically unlikely, but UNIQUE constraint catches it
- Multiple agents in same window: all share same window_uuid, each gets own agent registration
- Window ID persists but tmux pane restarted: correct behavior (identity survives restart)
- MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID set to invalid value: validate UUID format, reject with clear error

TESTS:

Unit Tests (tests/test_window_identity.py):
- test_window_id_created_on_first_registration: new UUID -> new window identity in DB
- test_window_id_reused_on_subsequent_registration: same UUID -> same display_name returned
- test_window_id_updates_last_active: re-registration updates last_active_ts
- test_window_display_name_unique_per_project: different windows get different names
- test_window_id_without_env_var: no MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID -> current behavior unchanged
- test_window_id_invalid_format: non-UUID value -> clear error message
- test_window_identity_in_message_metadata: sent messages include window_id and display_name
- test_rename_window: rename_window() updates display_name in DB
- test_expire_window: expire_window() marks identity as expired
- test_list_active_windows: list_window_identities() returns only non-expired windows

Integration Tests:
- test_multiple_agents_same_window: 3 agents with same window UUID -> all registered, shared identity
- test_window_persistence_across_sessions: register, shutdown, re-register -> same display_name
- test_cleanup_expired_windows: create window, set expires_ts to past, run cleanup -> removed

E2E Tests:
- test_full_window_lifecycle: set env var -> register -> send messages -> list windows -> verify identity
- test_window_identity_in_inbox: receive message -> inbox shows sender window_display_name

GitHub issue: #80","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:10:46.499297065Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T02:10:17.603336975Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T02:10:17.603306177Z","close_reason":"Implemented: WindowIdentity model, config, priority chain, 3 new MCP tools, message metadata enrichment, 11 tests. Commit 32afeab.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":9,"issue_id":"bd-1tz","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"REGISTER_AGENT PRIORITY CHAIN (explicit order):
1. Explicit agent_name parameter passed to register_agent() -> use as-is (highest priority)
2. MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID set + window identity exists in DB -> use window display_name
3. MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID set + window identity NOT in DB -> create new identity, use generated display_name
4. MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID NOT set + no explicit name -> auto-generate name (current behavior, lowest priority)

IMPORTANT: An agent that passes an explicit name should STILL be associated with the window identity (for tracking purposes), but the explicit name takes precedence for display.

ADDITIONAL TEST LOGGING REQUIREMENTS:
All tests should use Python logging with level=DEBUG. Each test should log:
  - Environment state: MCP_AGENT_MAIL_WINDOW_ID value (or 'not set')
  - Priority chain resolution: which step was used and why
  - DB state before/after: window_identities table contents
  - Agent registration result: agent_name, window_uuid, display_name
  - For lifecycle tests: created_ts, last_active_ts, expires_ts changes

Use pytest caplog fixture to capture and assert on log output:
  with caplog.at_level(logging.DEBUG):
      result = await tool('register_agent', ...)
  assert 'window identity created' in caplog.text
","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1v5","title":"Community feature ideas from Discord (GH #80)","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #80 filed by ratacat/Woodchip Daddy (2026-02-06). Five forward-looking feature ideas from Discord:

1. Persistent window-based agent identities — derive deterministic agent names from terminal/window ID via hash+wordlist
2. Topic threads / broadcast channels — broadcast to all agents in a "room" rather than directed to/cc/bcc
3. Reasoning / summarizer layer — periodic background summarization of communications
4. Push notifications for agents — wake up idle Claude Code or Codex agents on new messages
5. HUD for tmux panes — persist agent names and task summaries on tmux pane titles

## Owner Assessment
- Window-based identities: interesting but differs from current explicit create_agent_identity flow
- summarize_thread already exists as a tool; automatic periodic summarization is more ambitious
- Push notifications: unlikely near-term due to how Claude Code/Codex sessions work
- HUD integration: overlaps with ntm

## Status
Beads created for tracking (commits d9ed724, ec318e1). No implementation started.

## Priority by actionability
1. Window-based identities (LIQUID_MAIL_WINDOW_ID env var) — most feasible
2. Broadcast channels (pub/sub model) — moderate scope
3. Periodic summarizer (background task scheduler) — significant infra
4. Push notifications — blocked by external constraints
5. tmux HUD — integration concern, overlaps with ntm","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:03.697055443Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:17:03.697055443Z","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail#80","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["community","discussion","feature"]}
-{"id":"bd-26w","title":"Broadcast topic threads for all-agent visibility","description":"Issue #80 idea 2: Topic threads that broadcast to every agent in a project. Implemented as thin layer on existing messaging (NOT separate pub/sub infrastructure).

DESIGN DECISIONS:
- Broadcast is a parameter on send_message(), NOT a separate tool
- Topic is metadata on messages, NOT a subscription system
- broadcast=true internally expands "to" to all registered agents in project
- Topic filtering via existing resource parameters (no new subscription table)
- Respects contact_policy settings (agents can opt out of broadcasts)

Implementation:

1. Add broadcast parameter to send_message() (app.py):
   - New optional param: broadcast: bool = False
   - When broadcast=true AND to is empty: expand to = all registered agents in project (excluding sender)
   - When broadcast=true AND to is non-empty: error ("broadcast and explicit recipients are mutually exclusive")
   - Respect contact_policy: skip agents with contact_policy blocking sender

2. Add topic field to Message model (models.py):
   - New optional field: topic: Optional[str] = None
   - Stored in messages table as TEXT column (schema migration)
   - Indexed for efficient filtering: CREATE INDEX idx_messages_topic ON messages(project_id, topic)

3. Add topic filtering to fetch_inbox() (app.py):
   - New optional param: topic: Optional[str] = None
   - When set, filter inbox to only messages with matching topic
   - Resource parameter: resource://inbox/{Agent}?project=X&topic=blockers

4. Add fetch_topic() MCP tool:
   - fetch_topic(project_key, topic_name, limit=50) -> list of messages with that topic
   - Shortcut for fetching all messages in a topic thread regardless of recipient

5. Update send_message MCP tool schema:
   - Add broadcast: bool (default false) to tool input schema
   - Add topic: str (optional) to tool input schema
   - Document in tool description: "Set broadcast=true to send to all agents in the project"

EDGE CASES:
- Broadcast to 0 agents (only sender registered): warning log, message stored but no recipients
- Broadcast to 100+ agents: batch insert recipients, no performance issue with existing schema
- Archived/inactive agents: skip agents inactive for > 30 days in broadcasts
- Topic name validation: alphanumeric + hyphens only, max 64 chars
- ack_required with broadcast: each recipient must ack independently

TESTS:

Unit Tests (tests/test_broadcast.py):
- test_broadcast_expands_to_all_agents: 5 agents -> message delivered to all 4 others
- test_broadcast_excludes_sender: sender not in own recipient list
- test_broadcast_respects_contact_policy: agent with blocking policy skipped
- test_broadcast_with_explicit_recipients_errors: broadcast=true + to=["alice"] -> error
- test_broadcast_empty_project: only sender registered -> warning, message stored
- test_topic_stored_in_message: message with topic="blockers" -> topic field in DB
- test_topic_filtering_inbox: fetch_inbox with topic filter returns only matching messages
- test_topic_filtering_empty: fetch_inbox with non-existent topic returns empty
- test_fetch_topic_tool: fetch_topic returns all messages with matching topic
- test_broadcast_with_topic: broadcast + topic -> all agents receive topic-tagged message
- test_ack_required_broadcast: each recipient has independent ack status

Integration Tests:
- test_broadcast_then_fetch_inbox: send broadcast -> each agent sees it in inbox
- test_topic_thread_conversation: multiple messages same topic -> fetch_topic returns all in order
- test_broadcast_performance_50_agents: 50 registered agents -> broadcast completes in < 2 seconds

E2E Tests:
- test_full_broadcast_flow: register 3 agents -> broadcast message -> all 3 fetch inbox -> message present
- test_topic_workflow: agent1 broadcasts topic="architecture" -> agent2 fetches topic -> sees message

GitHub issue: #80","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-07T03:11:01.452762734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:59.711014817Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-26w","depends_on_id":"bd-1tz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:59.710945317Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":10,"issue_id":"bd-26w","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SCHEMA MIGRATION (must be explicit):
The new 'topic' column requires an ALTER TABLE migration:

  ALTER TABLE messages ADD COLUMN topic TEXT DEFAULT NULL;
  CREATE INDEX idx_messages_topic ON messages(project_id, topic);

This should be added to the SQLModel migration path. If using Alembic, create a new migration revision. If using auto-create, add the field to the Message model class and let SQLModel handle it.

SOFT DEPENDENCY ON bd-1tz: If bd-1tz (window identity) is implemented first, broadcast messages can include the sender's window_display_name in metadata, making broadcasts more readable in multi-agent grids. This is NOT a hard dependency — broadcasts work fine without window identity.

ADDITIONAL TEST LOGGING REQUIREMENTS:
All tests should log:
  - Broadcast expansion: 'Expanding broadcast to N agents: [agent1, agent2, ...]'
  - Contact policy filtering: 'Skipping agent X (blocked by contact_policy)'
  - Topic operations: 'Storing message with topic=blockers in project=Y'
  - Recipient delivery: 'Delivered broadcast msg-123 to 4/5 agents (1 blocked)'
  - Performance: 'Broadcast to 50 agents completed in Xms'

Use structured logging (JSON format) for machine-parseable test output.
","created_at":"2026-02-07T21:10:57Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2a3","title":"Prevent concurrent thread digest write races","description":"Thread digest appends in storage.py were unprotected; concurrent messages to same thread could interleave writes. Add a lock around digest append.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:59:28.429248204Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:59:37.774566998Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:59:37.774548883Z","close_reason":"Added lock to digest append","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":5,"issue_id":"bd-2a3","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented AsyncFileLock around thread digest append to prevent concurrent write interleaving.","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:59:32Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2bi","title":"Clear notification signal when inbox is read","description":"clear_notification_signal is defined but never used. Consider clearing signals after fetch_inbox/mark_message_read/mark-all-read to avoid stale notifications.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-20T04:25:44.983178631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T04:41:40.218069437Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T04:41:40.218026656Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-2c8","title":"Fix search_messages FTS5 hyphenated tokens (GH #67)","description":"SQLite FTS5 interprets hyphens as syntax, causing searches for POL-358 to fail. Need to auto-quote hyphenated tokens or handle them as literals.","notes":"TopazGrove starting work on this issue","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-21T20:14:33.684778397Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T20:22:53.571351730Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T20:22:53.571308970Z","close_reason":"Implemented auto-quoting of hyphenated tokens in FTS5 queries. Added _quote_hyphenated_tokens helper and integrated it into _sanitize_fts_query. Added comprehensive tests.","external_ref":"https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/mcp_agent_mail/issues/67","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
